Title: Elusive partners: a review of the auxiliary proteins guiding metabolic flux in flavonoid biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type IV chalcone isomerase-like (CHIL) protein; CHI-fold family
Common_Genes.Applied_Species: Arabidopsis; Japanese morning glory; hop; petunia; torenia; fern
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Ipomoea nil; Humulus lupulus; Petunia hybrida; Torenia hybrida; NA
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation; stress-related specialized metabolites; bioactive chalconoids
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generally promotes flavonoid metabolic flux by enhancing chalcone production and/or stabilizing chalconoid intermediates; loss of function reduces downstream flavonoids in multiple species
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutants; knockdown/silencing; overexpression; protein interaction assays; in vitro biochemical assays; engineered yeast pathway reconstitution
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHIL proteins are non-catalytic CHI-fold proteins that enhance flavonoid/chalconoid production despite lacking bona fide CHI activity. 2. In Arabidopsis and Ipomoea nil, CHIL loss of function reduces flavonols and/or proanthocyanidins and can reduce anthocyanin-associated pigmentation. 3. CHIL physically interacts with CHS and other pathway proteins, reduces aberrant CHS lactonization by-products such as CTAL, and increases naringenin chalcone/naringenin output. 4. In hops, CHIL homologs can stabilize open-ring chalconoids or enhance CHS/HIPT1L efficiency, favoring xanthohumol-pathway flux.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Correlates with flavonoid biosynthesis; high in flavonoid-accumulating tissues; in hops highly expressed in glandular trichomes of female flowers
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Arabidopsis seed/PA-associated tissues; Japanese morning glory flowers; hop female flower glandular trichomes
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; cytoplasm; endoplasmic reticulum surface when associated with metabolon partners
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CHS; CHI; isoflavone synthase; flavone synthase II; C4H; F3'H; HIPT1L
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper summarizing CHIL as an auxiliary/non-catalytic flux-guiding protein; not a bona fide enzyme and not a transcription factor. Includes both type III and IV CHI-fold proteins with species-specific functions.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful auxiliary factor for pathway engineering to improve flavonoid or chalconoid yields and reduce derailment of type III PKS products; potentially valuable for gene stacking with CHS/CHI/PT modules
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Species-dependent effects; anthocyanin impact may be weak or variable in Arabidopsis; mechanistic details remain unresolved
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Trichome-specific transcriptome in cannabis mentioned for candidate discovery; accession NA

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CHIL protein; type IV CHI-fold auxiliary protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function causes pale flowers and reduced anthocyanins, flavonols, and flavones; promotes early flavonoid biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Spontaneous mutant analysis; loss-of-function characterization; metabolite profiling; cross-species homolog silencing
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EFP encodes a non-catalytic CHIL protein in Ipomoea nil. 2. efp mutants show reduced anthocyanins and flavonols, including kaempferol glycoside, causing pale flowers. 3. Reduction of flavones suggests EFP acts with early pathway steps, likely in concert with CHS. 4. Silencing EFP homologs in petunia and torenia produced similar pale-flower phenotypes, supporting conserved function.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interpreted as enhancer of early flavonoid biosynthesis; bottleneck at CHS may divert p-coumaroyl-CoA toward caffeic acid derivatives when EFP is absent
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering flower color intensity and boosting early flavonoid flux
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function diverts flux toward caffeic acid derivatives such as chlorogenic acid and 1-O-caffeoyl glucoside

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bitter taste; pharmaceutical bioactivity including anticancer, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant potential
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chalconoid accumulation by binding intermediates and stabilizing the open-ring conformation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis; in vitro binding/function studies; engineered yeast pathway reconstruction
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HICHIL1 is a type III non-catalytic CHI-fold protein highly expressed in hop glandular trichomes. 2. It binds chalconoid intermediates and stabilizes the open-ring form. 3. This favors accumulation of prenylated chalcones over closed-ring flavonoids.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in glandular trichomes of female hop flowers
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts primarily through metabolite binding rather than direct enhancement of prenyltransferase activity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering of xanthohumol/prenylchalcone production
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May shift flux away from canonical flavonoids toward chalconoids

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances CHS and HIPT1L efficiency and boosts output of reconstituted chalconoid/flavanone pathways
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction assays; in vitro enzyme assays; engineered yeast pathway reconstitution
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HICHIL2 physically interacts with CHS and HIPT1L. 2. It increases CHS conversion rates and improves HIPT1L prenylation rates. 3. Addition of HICHIL2 enhances output of reconstituted DMX or naringenin pathways in yeast. 4. The effect suggests conserved CHIL regulation of CHS activity across land plants.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in glandular trichomes of female hop flowers
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Glandular trichomes; engineered yeast reconstitution context
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Type IV CHIL; review emphasizes conserved auxiliary enhancement of type III PKS flux
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong auxiliary engineering target for improving prenylchalcone and flavanone production in heterologous systems
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter branch allocation between chalconoids and flavonoids

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi suppression reduces major anthocyanins and alters flavonoid composition; proteins likely bind flavonoid intermediates and guide flux/transport/regulation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient RNAi silencing; metabolite profiling; X-ray crystallography; isothermal titration calorimetry
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fra a proteins are strawberry PR10/Bet v 1-like proteins implicated in flavonoid metabolism. 2. Silencing Fra a 1 and Fra a 3 reduces cyanidin 3-O-glucoside, pelargonidin 3-O-glucoside, and other upstream flavonoid metabolites. 3. Structural and calorimetric studies show binding to catechin and certain flavonols, supporting an auxiliary ligand-binding function. 4. Fra a proteins may act in metabolons, transport, flux control, and/or indirect regulation of PAL and CHS expression.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Fruit-associated; genotype-dependent abundance; some family members responsive to stress
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Proposed ER-associated metabolon; vacuolar/apoplastic shuttling model; possibly nucleus by inference
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Overexpression in wild type did not increase metabolite levels; suppression caused off-target CHS and PAL transcript reduction; large paralogous family complicates functional assignment
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning strawberry pigmentation and stress-linked flavonoid traits, but family redundancy and indirect effects require caution
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salicylic acid priming upregulates PR10 genes and increases proanthocyanidins; stress-responsive context
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression can broadly perturb hormones, WRKY TFs, ABC/MATE transporters, and stress responses

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances formation of cis-trans nepetalactol at expense of other diastereomers in coupled assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MLPL is a PR10-type auxiliary protein in iridoid biosynthesis. 2. In coupled assays with iridoid synthase and 8-oxogeranial, it promotes cis-trans nepetalactol formation and product selectivity. 3. The review cites MLPL as an example that PR10 proteins can guide stereochemistry and flux similarly to flavonoid-associated auxiliary proteins.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Occurs within an iridoid biosynthetic gene cluster
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in review as analogous auxiliary protein, not a flavonoid core gene strictly speaking but a summarized core example of auxiliary metabolic flux guidance
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary factor for stereoselective iridoid engineering

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Various land plants including Arabidopsis, Medicago, soybean
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Medicago sativa; Glycine max
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early core flavonoid pathway; chalcone-to-flavanone step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes naringenin chalcone to (2S)-naringenin; type II CHIs additionally convert isoliquiritigenin to liquiritigenin
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Early committed/core step downstream of CHS
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms flavanone precursor for downstream flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early pathway enzyme, but not presented as the review's main bottleneck compared with auxiliary CHIL control
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides enantiospecific formation of flavanone intermediates for downstream flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant tt5; biochemical assays; structural studies
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHI is the bona fide catalytic counterpart of CHIL proteins. 2. It produces stereospecific (2S)-flavanones from chalcones. 3. The review uses CHI as the benchmark to contrast the non-catalytic but flux-guiding role of CHIL.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: ER-associated metabolon interface via partner interactions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because CHI is a central summarized pathway gene in the review; not the main auxiliary focus
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core enzyme for flavonoid engineering, often stacked with CHS and auxiliary factors such as CHIL

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step of core flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses one p-coumaroyl-CoA with three malonyl-CoA units to produce a linear tetraketide that cyclizes to naringenin chalcone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the first dedicated flavonoid scaffold and supplies all downstream flavonoid branches
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major control/bottleneck point discussed in relation to promiscuity and CHIL-mediated rectification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Drives entry into flavonoid biosynthesis but can produce derailment by-products such as CTAL/BNY; CHIL improves productive chalcone output
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: tt4 mutant; in vitro enzyme assays; metabolon interaction studies; engineered yeast reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS is the upstream type III PKS that initiates flavonoid biosynthesis. 2. The review emphasizes CHS promiscuity, which can generate lactone by-products such as CTAL and BNY. 3. CHIL proteins rectify CHS output, enhancing naringenin chalcone formation and limiting aberrant lactonization. 4. CHS participates in ER-associated metabolons with CHI, CHIL, and CYP anchors.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosolic but associated with ER-surface metabolons
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CHI; CHIL; C4H; F3'H; isoflavone synthase; flavone synthase II; HIPT1L indirectly in hop context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central enzymatic partner whose promiscuity creates the need for auxiliary proteins
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential engineering node; combining CHS with CHIL can improve pathway productivity and reduce unwanted by-products
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity can divert carbon to CTAL/BNY by-products in vitro or heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Moonlighting epigenetic role suggested for CHS via MOS9 interaction, but not established as pathway catalytic regulation in this review

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Prenylates chalcone intermediate to form demethylxanthohumol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Naringenin chalcone or related chalconoid substrate; dimethylallyl diphosphate
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch-specific downstream decoration step after CHS
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor of xanthohumol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch enzyme for prenylchalcone formation; efficiency improved by HICHIL2
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes of female hop flowers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compound with anticancer, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant potential
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes prenylchalcone formation; activity enhanced by HICHIL2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HIPT1L is the hop prenyltransferase that forms DMX in the xanthohumol pathway. 2. HICHIL2 physically enhances HIPT1L catalytic efficiency. 3. This partnership supports efficient biosynthesis of prenylated chalcones in hop trichomes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in glandular trichomes of female hop flowers
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as pathway gene central to the review's hop example
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key target for xanthohumol engineering, especially with HICHIL2 co-expression

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late methylation step in hop chalconoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes of female hop flowers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for xanthohumol formation from DMX
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HLOMT1 methylates DMX to xanthohumol in hops. 2. The enzyme defines the final step producing the major bioactive prenylchalcone highlighted in the review.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as a core pathway enzyme in the hop chalconoid example
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful terminal-step gene for xanthohumol biofortification/bioproduction

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DABB protein; α+β barrel cyclase-like auxiliary enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Guides intramolecular C2-C7 aldol cyclization of OLS-derived tetraketide intermediate to form olivetolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: OLS-derived tetraketide intermediate from hexanoyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms essential precursor for cannabinoid alkylresorcinol moiety
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Crucial bottleneck-resolving step in phytocannabinoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes of aerial tissues/female flowers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables correct olivetolic acid formation when combined with OLS, resolving a missing step in cannabinoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Trichome-specific transcriptome candidate discovery; coupled biochemical assays; site-directed mutagenesis; engineered yeast reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OAC is a DABB protein identified from cannabis trichomes that catalyzes cyclization to olivetolic acid. 2. It acts without intrinsic PKS activity, guiding the correct aldol cyclization of OLS-derived intermediates. 3. Tyr72 and His78 are catalytic residues. 4. OAC illustrates a parallel auxiliary strategy to CHIL in type III PKS-derived metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OLS (functional coupling without required physical interaction)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a flavonoid gene but highlighted in review as analogous auxiliary protein; physical interaction with OLS is not required
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential for cannabinoid pathway reconstruction and improved cannabinoid precursor production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined OLS+OAC still yields some α-pyrone by-products, indicating missing additional control elements

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses dopamine and 4-hydroxyphenylacetaldehyde in an enantioselective Pictet-Spengler reaction to form norcoclaurine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms universal entry intermediate to BIAs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCS is the first bona fide catalytic PR10 recognized in specialized metabolism. 2. It catalyzes the entry condensation reaction of BIA biosynthesis. 3. The review uses NCS as precedent for catalytic neofunctionalization within PR10 proteins.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a major summarized PR10 metabolic enzyme in the review
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important entry enzyme for BIA engineering

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses tyramine and 3,4-dihydroxybenzaldehyde to form norbelladine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Galantamine used to treat cognitive decline/Alzheimer's disease
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates AA biosynthesis through norbelladine formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology search and biochemical characterization summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NBS is an NCS-like PR10 enzyme in daffodil alkaloid biosynthesis. 2. It catalyzes the condensation producing norbelladine, the entry precursor to AAs. 3. The review cites NBS as another example of catalytic PR10 neofunctionalization.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a summarized core PR10 metabolic enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful entry-point target for galantamine pathway engineering

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes deacetylation and intramolecular allylic rearrangement of salutaridinol 7-O-acetate to thebaine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Critical corrective step preventing aberrant by-products in pathway reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ensures correct flux to thebaine and improves downstream morphinan production in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein purification from latex; biochemical assay; pathway reconstitution in microbes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THS is a PR10 enzyme that catalyzes a step once thought spontaneous. 2. It converts salutaridinol 7-O-acetate to thebaine through deacetylation/rearrangement. 3. Including THS improves morphinan flux by avoiding erroneous side products.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cited as another auxiliary/corrective metabolic protein analogous to CHIL-type flux guidance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for microbial production of morphinans

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Facilitates isomerization of neopinone to codeinone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct substrate for codeinone reductase en route to morphine/codeine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Critical corrective step improving desired morphinan flux
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes correct isomerization and enhances flux to desired morphinan alkaloids in engineered microbes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NISO reduces the activation energy for neopinone-to-codeinone isomerization. 2. It provides the correct substrate for codeinone reductase. 3. Inclusion of NISO increases desired morphinan alkaloid titers in microbial systems.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Another PR10 example illustrating non-obvious catalytic correction of pathway flux
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for high-fidelity morphinan biosynthesis in engineered hosts

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PA biosynthesis genes; CHIL is described as coregulated with TT2 locus
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transparent testa mutant genetics summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Seed coat developmental program, NA explicitly
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as regulator of proanthocyanidin biosynthesis and as locus coregulated with CHIL in Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT2/MYB123 regulates proanthocyanidin biosynthesis in Arabidopsis. 2. CHIL is coregulated with TT2 and PA biosynthetic genes, linking auxiliary CHIL function to the PA regulatory network.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely MBW-type regulatory complexes by general knowledge, but not explicitly detailed here
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for seed coat PA traits

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transparent testa mutant genetics summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as TT locus regulatory factor controlling flavonoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT8/BHLH042 is identified as a regulator of anthocyanin accumulation in Arabidopsis. 2. It exemplifies the regulatory tier coordinating flavonoid biosynthetic output discussed in the review.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory target for pigmentation engineering

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Endothelial identity program affecting seed coat pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transparent testa genetics summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Determining factor in endothelial identity crucial to seed coat pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT16 encodes a MADS-box factor important for endothelial identity. 2. Proper endothelial identity is crucial for seed coat pigmentation and thus flavonoid deposition.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly enables pigment accumulation through developmental regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal seed coat pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be relevant for seed coat flavonoid traits but with developmental pleiotropy
